pages.raw_text:
A wire from Sidney Rooenhaupe
received by Dan Weaver announces
the suspicious opening Of the new
Cort theater 1t Chicago last Mon
day night. R0senhaupt 4 Weaver
are interested In the house.
Transportation companies report
q good business In the way of Ital-
ians, Greeks, Bulgarians, Rou-
manians and other foreigners re-
turning to their homes in southern
Europe to spend the winter. The
fare ranges from $98 to $50.


Edward q. Mccieave, first lieu
tenant of the Twenty-fifth infan-
| try, has joined his regiment at
Fort George Wright. He was de-
tached in charge of a detail TAX
ing rs military convicts from Ma
Mia, p. l., to Alcatrax prison at
San Francisco.


Maurice Seymour Of Seattle and
Miss Hazel Grimmer, daughter of
Mr.. and Mrs. J. M. Grimmer, will
be married this evening at the
brides home, 1843 Malion avenue.
w. p. Butler has gone to RosaIia
to inspect the stretch of state aid
road being built near there. He is
the superintendent of the good
roads commission for eastern
Washington.


A series Of six lectures on how
to tell stories to children will be
given by Miss Martha Sherwood of
the Cheney Normal school during
the winter under the auspices of
the teachers of the city. Her first
talk will be given at the South
Central high school tomorrow aft
ernoon at o'clock on, What Con-
stitutes Literary Story"


Bids are being received on the
new building to b eerected by the
Spokane Humane society and work
will begin at once on the basement
so that the structure may be ready
for use by spring.


Fred Morey Of Portland and
Miss Josephine Yates of this city
will be quietly married this even
ing at the home of the brides par
ents, 2028 Riverside avenue.


The opening dance Of the winter
season will be given this evening
at the Hall of the Doges by the
members of the country club.


The young men Of the American
Institute of Banking will give their
first dance tonight at the Elks
temple. The wives of the chief
banking officials of the city are
among the patronesses.


The history Of the seventeenth
century occupied the attention OF
the Cultus club at it's regular meet
ing yesterday at the home of Mrs.
George H. Martin, 01629 Cincin-
natl street


A lot 5ox142 on sprague avenue
between Napa and Magnolia has
been sold by w. X. Brewster to
Mrs. Laura Moss for $10,500. There
are three brick store rooms on the
ground that rent for SIS per month.


James WEIGH, a son Of the con-
tractor, Patrick WEIGH, and Miss |
Hester Lavell will be married |
November 6.


The fair being given In the old
postoffice building for the benefit |
OF the House Of the Good Shepherd |
is proving financial success. The |
second days receipts were $658.26,
making a total of $2451.76 for the
first two days.
